props.name: children
props.description: The contents of the card. Provide either `Card.*` subcomponents (for example `Card.Heading`, `Card.Description`, `Card.Metadata`) or any custom content. A card with no children will not render.

props.name: className
props.description: CSS class name(s) for custom styling.

props.name: padding
props.description: Controls the internal padding of the Card.

props.name: borderRadius
props.description: Controls the border radius of the Card.

props.name: as
props.description: The HTML element to render. Use `'section'` for **standalone** Cards (not inside a list of cards) so screen readers announce the Card as a labelled region; in that case, either `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` is required. Use the default `'div'` (and omit `Card.Heading`) when the Card is inside an `<li>` of a list of cards — the surrounding list already provides grouping.

props.name: icon
props.description: An Octicon or custom SVG icon to render.

props.name: aria-label
props.description: Accessible label for the icon. When omitted, the icon is treated as decorative.

props.name: alt
props.description: Alt text for accessibility. Defaults to empty string (decorative).

props.name: as
props.description: The heading level to render. Use on standalone Cards (with `as="section"` on the parent `Card`) only; do not use `Card.Heading` when the Card is inside an `<li>` of a list of cards.

props.name: children
props.description:
The descriptive text for the card. Rendered inside a `

` element so should be flowing text content.

props.name: children
props.description: The interactive control(s) to render in the top-right corner of the card, typically a single `IconButton` or `ActionMenu` trigger. When a card contains a menu, make sure the control's accessible name includes enough context to distinguish it from other cards (for example, 'Options for Project Alpha' rather than just 'Options').

props.name: children
props.description: The metadata content to render at the bottom of the card. Accepts any content, including plain text, icons, and other Primer components (for example a `Label`, `Octicon`, or any combination). Avoid using `RelativeTime` until its outstanding accessibility issues are resolved.
